primary_accounting_issue: Détermination, au sens d’IFRS 16, de l’éligibilité d’un bail renouvelable tacitement ou à durée indéterminée à l’exemption de court terme en fonction de la durée du bail et de sa période exécutoire

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence and scope rule by selecting IFRS 16 because it directly governs lessee lease term determination and the short-term lease exemption; no other candidate document provides an overlapping governing lease model for this issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty because the question is explicitly about lessee lease accounting and optional exemption eligibility, both of which are directly addressed in IFRS 16.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern the short-term lease exemption, the general lessee recognition model, and the determination and reassessment of lease term and enforceable period.

peripheral_authority.reason: This document discusses enforceable rights and contract duration in revenue contracts, but it does not govern lessee short-term lease exemption under IFRS 16.
This document addresses useful life of intangible assets and is not relevant to identifying lease accounting approaches for the short-term lease exemption.

treatment_families.family: Optional exemption model for short-term leases
General lessee recognition model for leases not accounted for using the short-term lease exemption

approaches.label: Expense recognition for short-term leases
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 16 provides a distinct optional model under which qualifying short-term leases are expensed rather than recognised on balance sheet.

approaches.label: Recognition of a right-of-use asset and lease liability
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 16 establishes the general lessee model requiring recognition of a right-of-use asset and lease liability when the short-term lease exemption is not applied.
